root.note: The **same tree** five times over, with notes from nothing to very tall. Nothing may overlap and the sheet must not grow with the note height.

children.note: Short notes stay next to their node.
Press Enter to add a sibling.
A medium note: two or three sentences of context that would clutter the node label itself. It wraps to a handful of lines and is still easy to read next to its node.
A medium note: two or three sentences of context that would clutter the node label itself. It wraps to a handful of lines and is still easy to read next to its node.
The branch note is short here; the tall one hangs on the middle leaf.

A note can be much taller than the node it belongs to:

- keyboard editing
- drag and drop
- collapse and expand
- Markdown descriptions
- auto-save to the browser
- JSON export and import
- SVG picture of the whole map
- no build step, no dependencies

The picture must stay compact anyway: this bubble sits in the gutter, so it may not push the branches below it downwards.

children.text: Should stay right below its sibling

children.note:
A note with no upper bound on its height. Everything below has to be drawn — a picture that quietly drops the end of a description is worse than a tall picture.

**Step 1.** Focus a node and press Alt+Enter to open its description.

**Step 2.** Write as much as the node deserves; Markdown is welcome.

**Step 3.** Press Ctrl+Enter to keep it, or Esc to throw it away.

**Step 4.** The 🗒 marker appears after the node text.

**Step 5.** Show graph draws the description next to its node.

**Step 6.** The bubble is exactly as tall as the text needs.

**Step 7.** Branches below it do not move — the bubble is in the gutter.

**Step 8.** Save As writes the whole document to a .json file.

children.text: Tall note on the branch itself
